query: these two modules feel way too tightly coupled — tell me concretely what kind of coupling it is and how to loosen the worst part
rationale: direct: grade the seam (connascence + coupling ladder) and weaken it

query: is a chained call like order.getCustomer().getAddress().getCity() a problem, and what do I do about it?
rationale: Law of Demeter / train wreck = content coupling

query: should this function take positional arguments or a named options object — which couples callers less?
rationale: connascence of position vs name; rule of degree

query: this helper module is just a grab-bag of unrelated init steps that run at startup — is that bad cohesion?
I pass a boolean flag into this shared method that switches its behavior — code smell?
design a plugin registry so new backends register themselves without editing the core
isolate my domain logic from Postgres behind an interface so I can swap the database
which module should own the nix provisioning code — it feels misfiled
rationale: near-miss: where a boundary goes -> orthogonal-pattern-guide
